channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Justice Department orders prosecutors to probe local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has ordered federal prosecutors to investigate state or local officials who they believe are interfering with the Trump administration's crackdown on immigration, saying they could face criminal charges, in an apparent warning to the dozens of so-called sanct
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A$AP Rocky's lawyers look for favorable jurors on charges he fired gun at former friend
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nearly a hundred jurors packed into a Los Angeles courtroom again on Wednesday with the possibility that they’ll serve on the trial of A$AP Rocky, who is charged with firing a gun at a former friend.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fast-moving Hughes Fire breaks out in Los Angeles County as California officials order evacuations
item.description: Officials ordered evacuations in the area surrounding the Hughes Fire on Wednesday morning, located near Castaic Lake in Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lynn Ban, star of Netflix reality show, dies at 52 after emergency surgery
item.description: Lynn Ban, who starred in Netflix's reality show "Bling Empire: New York," has died weeks after a skiing accident and emergency brain surgery.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KRON4

item.title: Nancy Leftenant-Colon, who was the first Black woman in Army Nurse Corps, has died
item.description: The first Black woman to join the U.S. Army Nurse Corps after the military was desegregated in the 1940s has died. She was 104.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 160 national security staffers are sent home as the White House aligns its team to Trump’s agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s national security adviser has sidelined about 160 National Security Council aides, sending them home while the administration reviews staffing and tries to align it with Trump’s agenda, administration officials told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Seth Rogen's 'The Studio' to open SXSW
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The SXSW Film & TV Festival will kick off March 7 with the world premiere of Seth Rogen and Evan Goldberg's “The Studio,” the Apple TV+ comedy series starring Rogen as a fictional Hollywood studio head.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Teen fatally shoots student, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Teen fatally shoots student, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KTLA 5

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Teen fatally shoots student, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court could revive lawsuit against Texas officer who shot motorist stopped for unpaid tolls
item.description: The Supreme Court seems inclined to revive a civil rights lawsuit against the Texas police officer who shot a man to death during a traffic stop in Houston over unpaid tolls.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Previously approved refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The U.S. refugee program is considered one of the most rigorous on the globe, providing a pathway to safe haven for those fleeing danger or who has faced persecution due to their race, religion, nationality or political views.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at school. Some families are now weighing school attendance
item.description: As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigrants in the U.S. illegally, some families are wondering if it is safe to send their children to school.
item.dc:creator: Olga R. Rodriguez, Moriah Balingit, Bianca Vázquez Toness And Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NYC subway conductor makes tragic discovery on train: dead woman
item.description: A woman between 50 and 60 years old and believed to be homeless was discovered dead on a subway train in New York City on Saturday, police say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, WGN 9

item.title: Ohio State VS. Notre Dame: Facts to know before the CFP Championship kickoff
item.description: The Buckeyes and Fighting Irish meet Monday night in the College Football Playoff national championship game at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, ABC 4

item.title: Biden endures painful but active last day of presidency
item.description: When President Biden walked through the Capitol on Monday with mere minutes left in his presidency, a reporter asked how he was feeling.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Planters hiring latest class of 'Nutmobile' drivers
item.description: Mr. Peanut is hiring. Planters is ready to bring in the 2025 class of brand ambassadors and drivers of their 26-foot peanut on wheels, the Nutmobile.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Costco workers authorize nationwide strike
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers nationwide voted Sunday to authorize a strike if a contract is not reached by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: Biden commutes sentence of Native American activist Leonard Peltier
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday commuted the life sentence of activist Leonard Peltier, who was controversially convicted of murdering two FBI agents, within the final hour of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Carrie Underwood sings 'America the Beautiful' a cappella after apparent tech issue at inauguration
item.description: An apparent technical issue didn't stop Carrie Underwood from delivering "America the Beautiful" during the second inauguration of President Trump on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Longtime Planned Parenthood head Cecile Richards dies at 67
item.description: Cecile Richards, the former president of Planned Parenthood, died at the age of 67 on Monday after a battle with cancer.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Bills will face Chiefs in AFC title game; Eagles will host the Commanders in NFC championship game
item.description: Josh Allen and the Buffalo Bills will take on Patrick Mahomes and the Kansas City Chiefs in the AFC championship game for their fourth playoff matchup in five years. Allen ran for two touchdowns and the Bills beat Lamar Jackson and the Baltimore Ravens 27-25 in a divisional game on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump inauguration to kick off 100-day rush
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to be sworn in at the Capitol on Monday, solidifying his stunning return to the White House and kicking off what is expected to be a busy 100-day rush focused on a handful of hot-button issues.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, ABC 4

item.title: As ceasefire takes hold, Hamas returns 3 Israeli hostages and Israel frees 90 Palestinian prisoners
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — After 15 months of collective grief and anxiety, three Israeli hostages left Hamas captivity and returned to Israel, and dozens of Palestinian prisoners walked free from Israeli jail, leaving both Israelis and Palestinians torn between celebration and trepidation as the ce
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Powell and the Clippers beat James and the Lakers 116-102 in Lakers' first visit to Intuit Dome
item.description: Norman Powell scored 22 points, James Harden added 21 points and 12 assists and the Los Angeles Clippers defeated LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers 116-102 on Sunday night in the Lakers' first visit to the new Intuit Dome.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The rise
item.description: The possibility of the U.S. outlawing TikTok kept influencers and users in anxious limbo during the four-plus years that lawmakers and judges debated the fate of the video-sharing platform. The moment its fans dreaded arrived late Saturday when the platform went dark in response to a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Heavy snow and frigid Arctic blast put 70 million across the US under winter storm warnings
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Tens of millions of residents along the East Coast are bracing for several inches of snow Sunday followed by dangerously cold temperatures that will grip much of the country from the Northern Plains to the tip of Maine.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 100 out-of-state homeless in Hawaii sent back to mainland families
item.description: Hawaii lawmakers claim programs from other states send homeless to the Islands, especially in the winter.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Moore, Fox 5

item.title: Why are lone homes left standing after the fires? It's not entirely luck
item.description: Emails and videos of burned buildings in Los Angeles next to those left standing have been flying back and forth among architects, builders and fire safety specialists around the world.
item.dc:creator: ED DAVEY and INGRID LOBET, Associated Press, ABC 4
